title: Thyme and rosemary disinfectant spray
subtitle: An isopropyl alcohol and white vinegar spray with thyme and rosemary essential oils for hard surfaces
excerpt: Isopropyl alcohol at 70% concentration is an effective surface disinfectant; combined with the antimicrobial properties of thyme and rosemary essential oils, this spray works on kitchen worktops, sinks, and bathroom hard surfaces. White vinegar adds acidity and degreasing power. Do not use on marble, granite, or natural stone surfaces.
sourceNotes: Isopropyl alcohol as a surface disinfectant: 70% concentration is more effective than 90%+ because the water content slows evaporation, giving the alcohol more contact time with the surface. CDC guidance on alcohol-based disinfectants. Thymol (from thyme EO) as an antimicrobial: EPA-registered disinfectant ingredient; effective against a range of bacteria and some viruses. Rosemary EO: also has some antimicrobial properties but lower thymol content than thyme EO. White vinegar (5% acetic acid): degreasing and deodorising; not a registered disinfectant but assists in removing grease and soils. Caution: do not use on marble, granite, quartz, or other natural stone; vinegar and alcohol can damage sealants and etch stone.

recipe.yieldDescription: Approximately 400 ml in a spray bottle
recipe.batchNotes: Scale by multiplying all weights. Fill into a 500 ml or larger spray bottle for larger batches.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Ready to use immediately. Label with the batch date.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 12 months in a sealed spray bottle. Alcohol-based cleaners are self-preserving. Discard if the spray develops an unusual smell beyond the normal alcohol-vinegar-herb character.

glossaryTerms.definition: The primary phenolic compound in thyme essential oil, responsible for its antimicrobial properties. Thymol is an EPA-registered antimicrobial active ingredient effective against a range of bacteria and some viruses. It is the active agent in some commercial disinfectant cleaners.

content.text: Alcohol works best as a surface cleaner at 70% strength. The water content at this level slows evaporation, giving the alcohol more contact time on the surface. Thyme essential oil contains

, a natural antimicrobial compound found in some commercial cleaners. White vinegar adds degreasing action. Do not use on marble, granite, quartz, or stone: the vinegar and alcohol can damage sealants and etch the surface. Good for sealed worktops, sinks, tiles, and bathroom fixtures.

items.prepNote: 90%+ grade; combined with water in this recipe it reaches approximately 70% concentration in the spray
Dilutes the isopropyl to an effective working concentration
Supporting antimicrobial note; adds a clean herbal scent

content.text: Add the essential oils to the isopropyl alcohol and stir briefly; alcohol dissolves essential oils without needing a separate emulsifier. Add the distilled water and white vinegar and stir to combine. Pour into the spray bottle and label with the batch date.

Spray onto hard surfaces: worktops, sinks, tiles, door handles, and bathroom fixtures. Leave for 30 seconds to 1 minute to allow the alcohol contact time to act. Wipe with a clean cloth. For food preparation surfaces, follow with a plain water rinse. Do not use on natural stone, marble, or granite. Do not use near an open flame; isopropyl alcohol is flammable.

Store in a sealed spray bottle away from heat and open flames; isopropyl alcohol is flammable. Keeps 12 months at room temperature. The alcohol acts as the preservative. Discard if the spray develops an unusual smell.
